mission.title: Install and Configure Cluster API (CAPI)
mission.description: Cluster API (CAPI) is a Kubernetes operator that provides declarative APIs and tooling to simplify the provisioning, upgrading, and management of multiple Kubernetes clusters. It is particularly useful for platform operators who need to automate cluster lifecycle management across various infrastructure environments. Key trade-offs include the complexity of setup and the requirement for a compatible infrastructure provider.

steps.title: Add the Cluster API Helm repository
steps.description:
This step adds the official Cluster API Helm chart repository to your Helm configuration.

```bash

helm repo add cluster-api https://charts.cluster-api.sigs.k8s.io

```

> This is the official repository for Cluster API.

steps.title: Install the Cluster API Custom Resource Definitions (CRDs)
steps.description:
CRDs are necessary for Cluster API to function correctly, as they define the custom resources used by the operator.

```bash

kubectl apply -f https://github.com/kubernetes-sigs/cluster-api/releases/download/v1.12.4/cluster-api.crds.yaml

```

> This command installs the required CRDs for version 1.12.4.

steps.title: Install the Cluster API controller
steps.description:
This step installs the Cluster API controller in the specified namespace, allowing it to manage cluster resources.

```bash

helm install cluster-api cluster-api/cluster-api --namespace kube-system --version 1.12.4

```

> This installs the Cluster API controller in the kube-system namespace.

steps.title: Create a Cluster API custom resource
steps.description:
This step creates a Cluster resource that defines the desired state of your Kubernetes cluster.

```bash

kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: cluster.x-k8s.io/v1beta1
kind: Cluster
metadata:
  name: my-cluster
  namespace: default
spec:
  infrastructureRef:
    apiVersion: infrastructure.cluster.x-k8s.io/v1beta1
    kind: MyInfrastructureProviderCluster
    name: my-infrastructure-cluster
EOF

```

> Replace 'MyInfrastructureProviderCluster' with your actual infrastructure provider.
commands:
kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: cluster.x-k8s.io/v1beta1
kind: Cluster
metadata:
  name: my-cluster
  namespace: default
spec:
  infrastructureRef:
    apiVersion: infrastructure.cluster.x-k8s.io/v1beta1
    kind: MyInfrastructureProviderCluster
    name: my-infrastructure-cluster
EOF

steps.title: Verify the Cluster API installation
steps.description:
This step checks the status of the Cluster API components to ensure they are running correctly.

```bash

kubectl get pods -n kube-system

kubectl get clusters

```

> You should see the Cluster API controller pod running.
> This command verifies that your cluster resource is created.

uninstall.title: Uninstall the Cluster API controller
uninstall.description:
This step removes the Cluster API controller and its associated resources. Be aware that this may lead to data loss if clusters are not properly deleted first.

```bash

helm uninstall cluster-api --namespace kube-system

kubectl delete -f https://github.com/kubernetes-sigs/cluster-api/releases/download/v1.12.4/cluster-api.crds.yaml

```

> This command removes the CRDs associated with Cluster API.

upgrade.title: Upgrade the Cluster API controller
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, ensure you back up your cluster resources and configurations. After upgrading, verify that the components are functioning correctly.

```bash

kubectl get clusters -o yaml > clusters-backup.yaml

helm upgrade cluster-api cluster-api/cluster-api --namespace kube-system --version 1.12.4

kubectl get pods -n kube-system

```

> Backup your cluster resources.
> Upgrade to the specified version.
> Verify that all pods are running after the upgrade.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'no matches for kind "Cluster" in version "cluster.x-k8s.io/v1beta1"'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This indicates that the Cluster CRD has not been installed or is not available.

**Fix:**
Reapply the CRDs using the command: kubectl apply -f https://github.com/kubernetes-sigs/cluster-api/releases/download/v1.12.4/cluster-api.crds.yaml

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'failed to create cluster: infrastructure provider not found'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This indicates that the specified infrastructure provider is not installed or configured correctly.

**Fix:**
Ensure that the infrastructure provider's Helm chart is installed and configured properly.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'unable to connect to the server: x509: certificate signed by unknown authority'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This indicates a problem with the kubeconfig or the API server's certificate.

**Fix:**
Check your kubeconfig file and ensure that the API server's certificate is valid.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'pod is in CrashLoopBackOff'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This indicates that one of the Cluster API controller pods is failing to start.

**Fix:**
Check the logs of the pod using: kubectl logs <pod-name> -n kube-system to diagnose the issue.

versionNotes.changes: Added support for new infrastructure providers and improved error handling.
versionNotes.deprecations: Deprecated the old API version 'v1beta1' for Cluster resources.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: If upgrading from v1.11.x, ensure to update your Cluster resources to the new API version.

resolution.summary: You should see the output of 'kubectl get clusters' showing your cluster resource with the desired state.
codeSnippets:
kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: cluster.x-k8s.io/v1beta1
kind: Cluster
metadata:
  name: my-cluster
  namespace: default
spec:
  infrastructureRef:
    apiVersion: infrastructure.cluster.x-k8s.io/v1beta1
    kind: MyInfrastructureProviderCluster
    name: my-infrastructure-cluster
EOF

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have a Kubernetes cluster running version 1.26 or higher and the necessary tools installed.
